zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Richard Starkey, Baron of Stratton於大約1201年出生在英國英格蘭爾斯特頓時，他的父親Richard Starkey32歲，母親Mrs Richard Starky31歲。他於1219年與Mrs Annore Starkey在英國英格蘭爾斯特頓結婚。他們至少是4個兒子的父母。他去世於1271年在他的家鄉享年71歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Richard Starkey, Baron of Stratton于大约1201年出生在英国英格兰爾斯特頓时，他的父亲Richard Starkey32岁，母亲Mrs Richard Starky31岁。他于1219年与Mrs Annore Starkey在英国英格兰爾斯特頓结婚。他们至少是4个儿子的父母。他去世于1271年在他的家乡享年71岁。

nameMeanings.name: Starkey,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from a lost or unidentified place, probably in Lancashire. This English surname has been established in Ireland (Dublin) since the 14th century.

English: variant of Starkin, itself perhaps from an unrecorded Old French personal name Starkin, a pet form of ancient Germanic names beginning in Stark- ‘strong’, such as Starc(w)ulf and Starcher, both of which appear in 1086 as names of Domesday Book tenants in East Anglia: Starcolf (Norfolk) and Starker (Suffolk). However, no medieval evidence for Starkin as a personal name or a surname has been found. An alternative possibility is a derivation from an unrecorded Middle English starking ‘strong one’ (see Stark ), but in the absence of any medieval evidence this is highly conjectural.
Altered form of German   Starke  , written thus to preserve the second syllable.

variants.name: Starkes
meanings: English (Hampshire and Sussex):
probably a variant of Starkey , with post-medieval excrescent -s.
variant of   Starks  .

variants.name: Sturkie
meanings: Possibly English: variant of   Starkey  .

variants.name: Starkie
meanings: English (Lancashire): variant of   Starkey  .

variants.name: Sturkey
meanings: English: possibly a variant of   Starkey  .

variants.name: Stark
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire) and English: nickname from Middle English stark ‘strong, sturdy; harsh, severe’ (Old English stearc).
German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): nickname for a strong, bold person, from Middle High German stark(e), German stark ‘strong, brave’.
Czech and Slovak (Štark): of German origin (see 2 above).

nameMeanings.name: Baron
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French, English, Dutch, Polish, Czech, Romanian, and Croatian; Breton (also Le Baron): from Old French barun, baron, Middle English barun, baroun, Middle Dutch baroen, Polish, Czech, Romanian, and dialectal Croatian baron ‘baron’ (i.e. a nobleman ranking below an earl and above a knight), a title of nobility which was in England also awarded to certain freemen of the cities of London and York and of the Cinque Ports. As a surname it is unlikely to be a status name denoting a person of rank. Generally, it referred to service in a baronial household or was acquired as a nickname by a peasant who had ideas above his station or applied to a man showing characteristics of a nobleman. See also 3 and 4 below, compare Barron and Lebaron .
French: habitational name from (Le) Baron, the name of several places in various parts of France. Compare   Lebaron  .
German: status name for a freeman or baron, from barūn ‘imperial or church official’, a loanword in Middle High German from Old French (see 1 above).

variants.name: Barron
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Jesus, Manuel, Francisco, Carlos, Miguel, Raul, Pedro, Ruben, Alfredo, Luis.

Scottish: status name from Scots baron, which in the Highlands denoted a small proprietary landholder. In Angus it alluded specifically to a tenant of one of the small baronies attached to the Abbey of Coupar Angus. The surname was taken by Scottish migrants to Ulster. Compare Baron .
Scottish: shortened form of McBarron, from Scottish Gaelic Mac a' Bharain ‘son of the baron’. See Baron and 1 above.

variants.name: Boron
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Krystyna, Edyta, Leszek, Mieczyslaw, Mieczyslawa, Wladyslaw.
Polish (Boroń): from a pet form of the personal names Borzysław or Borzymir, formed with the element bor ‘to fight’ (from Old Slavic boriti se).
French: habitational name from a place so called in Franche-Comté.

variants.name: Baro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Mario, Manuel, Ana, Angel, Carlos, Cesar, Fernando, Jesus, Jose, Orlando, Ramon, Rosalia.  Italian Guido, Angelo, Carmelo, Federico, Lucio.
Catalan (Baró): status name from baró ‘free man, baron’ (see Baron 1), or a habitational name from a place so named.
Hungarian (Báró): status name from báró ‘baron’. In the form Baró, it could be from a pet form of the personal names Barabás (see Barabas ) or Bertalan.

variants.name: Barona
meanings: Spanish: from the feminine form of Barón ‘baron’ (see Baron ), applied as a nickname or as an occupational name for a member of the household of a baroness.
Italian: probably from the feminine form of   Barone  . The name is concentrated in Sondrio province (Lombardy).

variants.name: Barren
meanings: English: probably a variant of   Baron   or   Barran  .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Korean Kyong, Kyong Suk, Young, Chae, Chi Young, Hang, Kil, Rin, Sang Hun, Chang, Chong, Insoo, Kum, Sun Young, Yom, Young Hwan.  Cambodian Kiwan, Oeurn, Phoeun, Pich, Saroeuth, Seung, Sophath, Sophea, Yoeun, Phan, Son.  Indian Shanti, Harendra.
Indian (Uttar Pradesh): Brahmin name, from Sanskrit om, the Hindu sacred and mystic syllable uttered at the beginning of prayers.
Cambodian: written ឱម, corresponding to the exclamation of solemn affirmation, of Sanskrit origin (see 1 above), chanted by Buddhist monks.

variants.name: Oum
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Southeast Asian Heang, Hye, Kam, Kan, Kheng, Khin, Kun, Sang, Sok, Phong, Yom, Yoo, Youn, Cuong, Phal, Samnang, Tin.  Cambodian Sokha, Chheang, Choeun, Moeun, Roeun, Sambath, Samoeun, Savuth, Soeun, Sopheap.
Cambodian: written អ៊ុំ, from a Khmer word denoting an elder sibling of one's parent (i.e. ‘uncle’ or ‘aunt’). Compare   Om   3 and   Um   2.

nameMeanings.name: Stratton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from any of several places called Stratton or Stretton, almost all named with Old English strǣt ‘paved road, Roman road’ + tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’. Stratton in Cornwall, which may also be a partial source of the surname, probably has as its first element Cornish stras ‘valley’.

English: variant of Sturton, a habitational name from Sturton le Steeple (Nottinghamshire), Great Sturton (Lincolnshire), Sturton by Stow (Lincolnshire), or possibly Sturton (Northumberland), all of which placenames share the same etymology and early spellings as 1 above.

Scottish: habitational name from Straiton (Ayrshire), Straiton in Liberton (Midlothian), or South Straiton in Logie (Fife), all named with Old English strǣt ‘street, Roman road’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’.

variants.name: Stretton
meanings: English (Leicestershire): variant of   Stratton   1.

variants.name: Strutton
meanings: English (Middlesex): variant of   Stratton  .

variants.name: Straiton
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from Straiton (Ayrshire), Straiton in Liberton (Midlothian), or South Straiton in Logie (Fife), all named with Old English strǣt ‘paved road, street’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Compare Stratton .

nameMeanings.name: Sir
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Old Norse, originally a byname meaning ‘bride, wife’; in mythology the name of Thor's golden-haired wife.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Richard Starkey, Baron of Stratton was born about 1201, in Stretton, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Richard Starkey, was 32 and his mother, Mrs Richard Starky, was 31. He married Mrs Annore Starkey in 1219, in Stretton, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons. He died in 1271, in his hometown, at the age of 71.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Richard Starkey, Baron of Stratton est né environ 1201, à Stretton, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Richard Starkey, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Mrs Richard Starky, avait 31 ans. Il a épousé Mrs Annore Starkey en 1219, à Stretton, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils. Il est décédé en 1271, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 71 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Richard Starkey, Baron of Stratton um 1201 in Stretton, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Richard Starkey 32 und seine Mutter Mrs Richard Starky 31. Er hat 1219 in Stretton, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom Mrs Annore Starkey geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne. Er starb 1271 im Alter von 71 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Richard Starkey, Baron of Stratton è nato il circa 1201 nel Stretton, Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Richard Starkey, aveva 32 anni e il suo madre, Mrs Richard Starky, aveva 31 anni. Lui sposato Mrs Annore Starkey nel 1219, nel Stretton, Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli. Morì nel 1271, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 71 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Starkey, Baron of Strattonが約1201年にイギリスイギリスチェシュアイアストレットオンで生まれた時，父Richard Starkeyは32歳，母Mrs Richard Starkyは31歳であった。1219年にイギリスイギリスチェシュアイアストレットオンでMrs Annore Starkeyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子の親であった。彼は1271年に故郷で71歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Starkey, Baron of Stratton(이)가 대략 1201년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 스트레턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard Starkey(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Mrs Richard Starky(은)는 31세였다. 그는 1219년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 스트레턴에서 Mrs Annore Starkey(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1271년 그의 고향에서 71세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Richard Starkey, Baron of Stratton nasceu aproximadamente 1201, em Stretton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Richard Starkey, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Mrs Richard Starky, tinha 31 anos. Ele casou-se com Mrs Annore Starkey em 1219, em Stretton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos. Ele faleceu em 1271, em sua cidade natal, com 71 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Richard Starkey, Baron of Stratton родился около 1201 г. в местности стреттон, чешир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Richard Starkey было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Mrs Richard Starky, 31. Он женился на женщине по имени Mrs Annore Starkey в 1219 г. в местности стреттон, чешир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына. Он умер в 1271 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 71 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Richard Starkey, Baron of Stratton nació aproximadamente 1201, en Stretton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Richard Starkey, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Mrs Richard Starky, tenía 31 años. Se casó con Mrs Annore Starkey en 1219, en Stretton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos. Murió en 1271, en su pueblo, a la edad de 71 años.
